mechanism_type: Lewis acid and HCl mediated partial reduction via imidoyl chloride/aldimine intermediates
reactant_pattern_text: aliphatic or aromatic nitrile + HCl + SnCl2
common_conditions: anhydrous HCl and stannous chloride followed by aqueous hydrolysis
description_short: Classical partial reduction of nitriles to aldehydes through imidoyl chloride or aldimine tin complexes followed by hydrolysis.

transformation_type: umpolung conjugate acylation of Michael acceptors by aldehydes
mechanism_type: cyanide or NHC/thiazolium catalyzed acyl-anion equivalent formation and Michael addition
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde + activated alkene/enone/enoate/nitrile under nucleophilic catalysis
common_conditions: nucleophilic catalysis with cyanide or thiazolium salts, often above room temperature
description_short: Umpolung addition of aldehydes to Michael acceptors using cyanide or thiazolium catalysis to furnish gamma-dicarbonyl products.

mechanism_type: base-induced rearrangement of onium ylides via radical-pair or concerted pathways
reactant_pattern_text: quaternary ammonium or sulfonium salt + strong base
product_pattern_text: rearranged tertiary amine or sulfide after [1,2]-shift
common_conditions: generation of nitrogen or sulfur ylides under basic conditions followed by migratory rearrangement
description_short: Base-promoted rearrangement of ammonium and sulfonium ylides that transfers carbon substituents adjacent to heteroatom centers.
